title: Pick a lane: why niching down speeds you up
dek: Serving everyone is the slowest possible route to being known for anything.
heroAlt: Pick a lane: why niching down speeds you up

blocks.html: The fear is always the same. If I focus on one client type, I lose everyone else. So agents stay general, their message stays generic, and nobody in any group thinks of them first. The fear of missing deals guarantees it.

A niche is not a fence. It is a flag. Plant it for corporate relocations, or downtown professionals with large dogs, or first-time luxury renters, and something counterintuitive happens: the specific reputation attracts general business. People hire the specialist and then hand them everything else too.

blocks.text: Nobody refers a generalist. People refer the person for exactly this.

items.title: Follow your unfair advantage.
items.html: A past career, a language, a building you know cold, a network you already have. The best niche is the one where you start ahead.

items.title: Check the volume.
items.html: The group needs to move often and exist at scale in your market. Passion without turnover is a hobby.

items.title: Name it publicly.
items.html: Put it in every bio this week. The niche does not exist until strangers can repeat it.

blocks.html: Finish this sentence and post it: I specialize in helping ___ find luxury apartments in ___. Discomfort with the narrowness is usually the sign it is finally specific enough.
Lead Gen Mastery, Course 3, covers niche targeting as the multiplier on every other lead channel you run.

blocks.lead: Course 3 shows how a niche multiplies lead flow.
